title: Pan Seared Greek Skirt Steak Skewers
description: A fermented, flaky, solo-dinner Greek dish featuring skirt steak, shio-cure, and Mediterranean-friendly flavors
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together shio cure, olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, oregano, salt, and pepper to make the marinade.
Add the skirt steak pieces to the marinade and toss to coat, then cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat, threading the marinated steak pieces onto skewers if using wooden skewers, soak them in water for 30 minutes before threading.
Remove the steak from the marinade, letting any excess liquid drip off, and brush the grill or grill pan with oil to prevent sticking.
Grill the steak skewers for 3-4 minutes per side, or until they reach an internal temperature of 130 degrees for medium-rare.
Let the steak rest for 5 minutes before slicing and serving.
Serve the steak skewers hot, garnished with lemon wedges and chopped fresh oregano if desired.
